metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete invocation path for the criterion arm of `run_case`: document that it calls `passes.pass1_chunk(runner, cfg, plan=case['input'], chunk=[descriptor])` directly (no `case_store`/`create_ticket` scaffolding), and clarify how `LLMConfig` is constructed for this arm.
findings.finding: The plan says 'a criterion runs as its Pass-1 finder against a fixture' but does not resolve that the existing run_case mechanism (case_store + rebar.create_ticket + disposable git repo) is structurally inapplicable to plan-review criteria. pass1_chunk takes a plain plan string and a criterion descriptor directly — no ticket store, no create_ticket, no git repo. The plan's 'reconcile the invocation model' note names the problem without specifying what the new dispatch arm actually calls, leaving the implementer to discover a non-trivial structural mismatch.
findings.location: What section / eval_solver.run_case dispatch / AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'The criterion arm of run_case calls passes.pass1_chunk(runner, cfg, plan=case["input"], chunk=[criterion_descriptor]) directly — no disposable store or ticket scaffolding is needed. LLMConfig is constructed from the runner's config (as in _live_solver). The case_store/create_ticket path is only for the 3 existing op-backed arms.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the per-criterion invocation model resolution in the plan: document whether run_case's new criterion arm calls passes.pass1_chunk directly (plan-text fixture, no store) or adapts the existing store-seeding path, and define the fixture payload key(s) (e.g. `plan:` vs `ticket_context:`) for criterion eval.yaml cases.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel per-criterion invocation pathway in eval_solver.run_case whose design is unresolved. The existing whole-op run_case arms (completion-verifier / ticket-quality / spec-alignment) seed a disposable rebar store with ticket objects and invoke full agentic ops. A per-criterion eval instead needs to invoke passes.pass1_chunk(runner, cfg, plan=<fixture_plan>, chunk=[criterion_descriptor]) — a fundamentally different call shape. The plan acknowledges the mismatch ('resolve the T3 advisory') but does not specify the resolution: does run_case grow a new arm that calls pass1_chunk directly, or does it adapt the store-seeding path, or does it introduce a third mechanism? Without this decision, implementors face an under-specified architectural choice at the seam between the two existing patterns.
findings.location: ## What — 'reconcile the invocation model (the criterion runs as its Pass-1 finder against a fixture); resolve the T3 advisory'; AC: 'eval_solver.run_case runs an arbitrary criterion prompt_id… the invocation model is documented'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Invocation model' subsection under ## What: 'A new run_case arm for criterion prompt_ids calls passes.pass1_chunk(runner, cfg, plan=case["plan"], chunk=[criterion_descriptor_by_id(prompt_id)]) directly — no rebar store needed. Fixture cases carry a `plan:` key (the plan text) plus `expect: finding|pass`. The existing store-seeding arms (completion-verifier / ticket-quality / spec-alignment) are unchanged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the T3 advisory in the plan body (not just in AC1): specify the invocation model for a per-criterion prompt — which call (e.g., `pass1_chunk` single-turn vs. agentic), what inputs the case dict must carry (`plan` text field?), and what the runner/cfg seam is — so an implementer has a complete protocol, not just a documented outcome.
findings.finding: The plan references 'resolve the T3 advisory (the existing solver's invocation model vs a per-criterion prompt)' as explicit work in the What section, but provides no description of what the resolution IS — only that one must happen. The T3 advisory is a referenced decision rule with no defined outcome, leaving the implementer to independently determine what the correct invocation model for a per-criterion prompt is (single-turn pass1_chunk call? agentic? something else?). AC1 only verifies the result ('the invocation model is documented'), not its content.
findings.location: § What — first bullet: 'resolve the T3 advisory (the existing solver's invocation model vs a per-criterion prompt)'; AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the output schema that `eval_solver.run_case` produces for the new criterion arm (e.g., `plan_review_findings` / P1Output), document which `_fired()` branch applies, and confirm it is registered in `contracts.py` — co-locate this with the dispatch protocol in `eval_solver.py`.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the new criterion eval dispatch runs a criterion 'as its Pass-1 finder against a fixture', but does not define what output schema that dispatch path produces (plan_review_findings / P1Output vs. review_result vs. something else). The existing `_fired()` predicate in eval_scorers branches on whether `verdict` is present (completion_verdict path) or falls back to `bool(findings)` (review_result/plan_review_findings path). Without specifying the output schema for the new criterion dispatch, an implementer cannot know which branch applies, whether the schema is registered in contracts.py, or how downstream scorers (recall, no_fire) interpret the output.
findings.location: ## What — first bullet; AC1 (`eval_solver.run_case` open dispatch); `src/rebar/llm/eval_solver.py` (lines 66-112), `src/rebar/llm/eval_scorers.py` `_fired()` (lines 73-79)
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's `eval_solver.run_case` open-dispatch section, add: 'A criterion prompt_id not in the closed 3-id set dispatches via `pass1_chunk` (or equivalent single-criterion call), producing a `plan_review_findings` (P1Output) output; `_fired()` uses the `bool(findings)` branch (no `verdict` key); only `expect: finding` and `expect: pass` fixtures are valid for criterion eval cases.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `expect` vocabulary for per-criterion eval fixture cases (must-fire → `expect: finding`; must-not-fire → `expect: pass`; discrimination fixtures → specify whether `high_validity`/`low_validity` etc. apply) and co-locate this mapping in the plan alongside the calibration metric definitions in AC3.
findings.finding: AC3 says 'the eval verdict vocabulary is reconciled with the fixture expect vocabulary', but neither the plan body nor the ACs define the mapping rule. The existing ALLOWED_EXPECTS enum contains `finding`, `fail`, `pass`, `high_validity`, `low_validity`, `high_impact`, `low_impact`, `high_novelty`, `low_novelty`. The plan uses 'must-fire/must-not-fire + discrimination fixtures' as terminology, but does not specify which `expect` values are valid for a per-criterion eval case, nor how the calibration metrics (recall = fraction of must-fire fixtures it fires on) map to the scorer predicates `_recall`/`_no_fire` that dispatch on FIRE_EXPECTS/NOFIRE_EXPECTS. The decision rule ('criterion fires' = `bool(findings)` for a plan_review_findings output) is named but not co-located with the schema.
findings.location: AC3 ('reconcile the eval verdict vocabulary with the fixture expect vocabulary'); `src/rebar/llm/eval_scorers.py` FIRE_EXPECTS/NOFIRE_EXPECTS (lines 32-33); plan § What, third bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling for `rebar criteria eval`: unknown ID, missing fixture file, empty fixture subsets (must-fire or must-not-fire), LLM timeout/failure mid-run, and vocabulary reconciliation failures — including the user-visible message and exit code for each.
findings.finding: The `rebar criteria eval <id>` CLI command's non-happy-path error states are unaddressed: missing criterion ID, absent `.rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml` fixture file, empty must-fire or must-not-fire fixture sets, LLM timeout/failure mid-run, and irreconcilable verdict/expect vocabulary are all unspecified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 / AC3 / 'reconcile the verdict vocabulary'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add failure/invalid/empty-path acceptance criteria for `rebar criteria eval <id>`: cover unknown criterion ID (error exit), malformed fixture YAML (user-readable error), empty must-fire or must-not-fire fixture sets (metric defined/skipped gracefully), and LLM timeout during N-run stability.
findings.finding: The new `rebar criteria eval <id>` CLI flow has no failure/invalid/empty-path testing specified: unknown criterion ID, malformed `.rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml`, empty fixture sets, LLM timeout during N-run stability, and vocabulary mismatch between `verdict` and `expect` are all unaddressed in the plan's test strategy.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'rebar criteria eval <id> exits with a descriptive error when <id> is not found in `.rebar/evals/`, when the YAML is unparseable, or when must-fire/must-not-fire fixture lists are empty; recall/false-accept are reported as N/A rather than crashing.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that 'N-run stability' for `rebar criteria eval` reuses the fixture spec's `epochs` / `at_least(k)` mechanism (i.e., calls `run_eval` or the same aggregation loop) rather than introducing a separate --runs parameter.
findings.finding: The plan mentions 'N-run stability' for `rebar criteria eval <id>` without specifying whether N is taken from the fixture spec's `epochs` field (which already governs run count in run_eval) or a separate CLI parameter, and how 'stability' is reported — leaving ambiguity about whether this is a new concept or a reuse of the existing at_least(k)/epochs mechanism.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit sequencing note: (1) extend run_case + reconcile verdict/expect vocabulary first as a standalone testable step, (2) wire CLI after runner is verified, (3) add calibration rendering last; and state whether this ticket is blocked on Story #1 or can partially proceed without it.
findings.finding: The plan has no stated sequencing or thin-vertical-slice MVP — the three interdependent pieces (runner extension → CLI wiring → calibration rendering) are presented as a flat list with no de-risking order, and the Story #1 dependency is implicit rather than gated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define N (e.g., N=5) and the stability criterion (e.g., all N verdict outputs must agree, or pass-rate variance < 5%) in AC2 so the acceptance bar is deterministic.
findings.finding: 'N-run stability' in AC2 does not define N (the number of runs) or a stability criterion (e.g., all N runs must agree, or variance < threshold), leaving the implementer to choose an arbitrary value with no acceptance bar.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'runs must-fire/must-not-fire + discrimination fixtures live from `.rebar/evals/` with N-run stability'
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC2 to: '…with N-run stability (N=5; a fixture is considered stable if all 5 runs return the same verdict)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'the T3 advisory' with an inline description of the actual constraint it encodes (e.g., 'the existing solver passes a composed prompt while per-criterion eval needs to call the criterion's own prompt template directly — these must be reconciled by X approach').
findings.finding: The plan references a 'T3 advisory' without defining it, leaving the implementer unable to know what design constraint or prior decision must be resolved.
findings.location: What section: 'resolve the T3 advisory (the existing solver's invocation model vs a per-criterion prompt)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC1 to verify the invocation model conflict is *resolved* (e.g., 'the T3 advisory is resolved and the reconciled invocation model is documented'), not merely that some model is documented.
findings.finding: The plan body describes 'resolve the T3 advisory (the existing solver's invocation model vs a per-criterion prompt)' as explicit work, but AC1 only verifies that 'the invocation model is documented' — documentation does not confirm resolution of the architectural conflict, leaving the resolution outcome uncovered by any acceptance criterion.
findings.location: What section ('resolve the T3 advisory') vs AC1 ('the invocation model is documented')
findings.suggested_fix: AC1 revised: '`eval_solver.run_case` runs an arbitrary criterion prompt_id (built-in or project); the T3 advisory (invocation model vs per-criterion prompt) is resolved and the reconciled model is documented.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that existing tests for the closed 3-id dispatch in `eval_solver.run_case` are updated/removed, and that at least one behavioral test verifies the new open-dispatch path routes a project criterion prompt_id to the correct evaluator.
findings.finding: The closed 3-id dispatch in `eval_solver.run_case` is being replaced with open dispatch (the primary changed behavior), but no corresponding modify/remove-test work for the existing tests of that dispatch is mentioned anywhere in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify N-run stability count as a configurable parameter (e.g., `--runs N` CLI flag with a documented default) rather than a hardcoded value.
findings.finding: The N-run stability parameter 'N' is referenced as a concept but not specified as configurable — it may end up hardcoded, making it difficult to tune without a code change.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `--runs <int>` flag to `rebar criteria eval` with a default (e.g., 5), and thread it through `eval_solver.run_case` so it's overridable without a code change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section capturing the open-dispatch invocation model, the criterion prompt_id contract, and the verdict/expect vocabulary reconciliation decision.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new open-dispatch pattern for criterion-aware eval running (replacing a closed 3-id hardcoded dispatch) and a new calibration pipeline stage, but mentions no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc capturing this architectural decision.
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/0NNN-criterion-eval-dispatch.md` covering: (1) why closed dispatch was replaced with open dispatch, (2) the invocation model (how a criterion's Pass-1 prompt is called against a fixture), (3) the verdict↔expect vocabulary mapping table.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in AC1 (or a dedicated compatibility note) that the 3 existing hardcoded IDs must still work after the dispatch is opened, and describe the backward-compatibility contract.
findings.finding: The plan changes eval_solver.run_case's dispatch behavior (from closed 3-id set to open) but does not acknowledge existing callers that pass those 3 hardcoded IDs or document backward-compatibility expectations.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to AC1: 'Existing built-in IDs (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment) continue to resolve identically; they become instances of the general path, not special cases.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale for extending `run_case` rather than introducing a standalone criteria-eval entry point (e.g., it preserves the existing `solve(prompt_id, case) -> dict` contract that `run_eval`'s `_live_solver` already injects, so the calibration loop reuses the existing epoch/coverage/gating aggregation without duplication).
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for extending run_case (vs. a standalone criteria-eval function that bypasses run_case entirely). The existing run_case is op-oriented scaffolding; the plan-review criterion path is structurally different enough that an implementer could reasonably ask whether to extend run_case or introduce a separate entry point.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
